LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT REGAN PLUIM'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 128.5 AND 128.7. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS THE FOLLOWING TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT REGAN PLUIM'S "MOTION FOR SANCTIONS UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 128.5 AND 128.7" IS DENIED. THE COURT HAS ALREADY THOROUGHLY REVIEWED THE COMPLAINT IN DECIDING PRIOR MOTIONS IN THIS CASE. IF DEFENDANT BELIEVES PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED, SHE IS FREE TO FILE A MOTION TO THAT EFFECT. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B, ULMER JR., CLERK: R. WOODS, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU) ***AFTER HEARING: ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

Off Calendar Oct-26-2023 Continued To Dec-20-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

STATUS CONFERENCE RE: DISCOVERY
Off Calendar Oct-18-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Regan Pluim's "special motion to strike plaintiffs' complaint under CCP 425.16" is denied.

Plaintiff Kevin Pluim was married to Regan; plaintiff Samantha Garden is Kevin's new partner. Plaintiffs' complaint pleads that Regan "engaged in a campaign of abuse, harassment, and intentional conduct" against them and their children. (Id. at 2:13-14.) This allegedly included calling Samantha a "whore" and "cunt homewrecker" to third parties and telling them she is "not safe" around children. (See, e.g., id. at 5:15-20, 7:22-26.) Regan's anti-SLAPP motion targets three of the complaint's 12 counts: defamation, public disclosure of private facts and false light.

"Section 425.16" sets "a two-step process for determining whether an action is a SLAPP. First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the challenged cause of action is one arising from protected activity. If the court finds that such a showing has been made, it must then determine whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Navellier v. Sletten (2002) 29 Cal.4th 82, 88.)

For the notion that the three causes of action arise from protected activity, Regan relies on her divorce proceeding with Kevin and CCP 425.16(e)(2), which regards statements "made in connection with an issue under consideration or review by a judicial body." (Memo. 12:5-7.) However, Regan fails to make the required first-step showing. (Navellier, 29 Cal.4th at 88.)
No one suggests that the vile epithets and accusations occurred in court or in court filings. Regan instead argues that whether Samantha is a "whore" or "cunt homewrecker" is an issue under consideration in the divorce proceeding, but her memorandum cites nothing in the court record for that assertion. (Memo. 12:24-28.) CCP 425.16 is not an open invitation to vile epithets and accusations when a court case begins.

The two opinions Regan relies on do not help her. Neville v. Chudacoff (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1255, 1259 regarded a lawyer's letter "directly related" to claims an employer would file against an employee in a court case. Annette F. v. Sharon S. (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 1146, 1161 regarded domestic violence allegations "directly at issue" in a judicial proceeding.

Because Regan fails to make her "threshold showing," the second CCP 425.16 step - plaintiffs' "probability of prevailing" on their claims - is not reached. (Navellier, 29 Cal.4th at 88.)
Counsel for the prevailing party to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

STATUS CONFERENCE RE: DISCOVERY

Off Calendar Jun-22-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Defendant REGAN PLUIM's Motion For Sanctions Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.5 And 128.7.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The court adopts the following tentative ruling:

Defendant REGAN PLUIM's "motion for sanctions under Code of Civil Procedure section 128.5 and 128.7" is denied. The court has already thoroughly reviewed the complaint in deciding prior motions in this case. If defendant believes plaintiffs' claims are time-barred, she is free to file a motion to that effect.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

***After hearing: order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Allowed To Conduct Limited Discovery
Off Calendar Jun-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ppearances:

Michon Spinelli, in court, ROPERS MAJESKI PC, (650) 364-3200, for Plaintiffs.

Kyle Withers, via Zoom, LUBIN OLSON & NIEWIADOMSKI LLP, (415) 981-0550, for Defendant.

Plaintiff KEVIN PLUIM and SAMANTHA GARDEN's motion to be allowed to conduct limited discovery.

Ruling - A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the court takes Plaintiff KEVIN PLUIM and SAMANTHA GARDEN's "motion to be allowed to conduct limited discovery, under submission.

This case involves an acrimonious marital breakup. Plaintiff's pled that defendant "engaged in a campaign of abuse, harassment, and intentional conduct" against them and their children. Defendant filed a motion to strike much of the complaint under Code of Civil Procedure 425.16, the anti-SLAPP statute. "Section 425.16" sets "a two-step process for determining whether an action is a SLAPP. First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the challenged cause of action is one arising from protected activity. If the court finds that such a showing has been made, it must then determine whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Navellier v. Sletten (2002) 29 Cal.4th 82, 88.) In order to demonstrate "a probability of prevailing" on their claims (id.), plaintiffs say they need discovery. This is authorized by CCP 425.16(g): "The court, on noticed motion and for good cause shown, may order that specified discovery be conducted?" (See also Lafayette Morehouse, Inc. v. Chronicle Publishing Co. (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 855, 868 ["plaintiff must be given the reasonable opportunity to obtain [] evidence through discovery before the motion to strike is adjudicated"].) The specified discovery this court orders goes directly to matter defendant seeks to strike: "(1) the extent to which Defendant has kept, maintained, used, stored or otherwise disseminated private and personal information about Plaintiffs; and (2) the nature, substance, and extent of Defendant's communications about Plaintiffs and their families to third parties."

This discovery shall be completed by September 11, 2023. The court will hold a status conference September 18, 2023.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

****After Hearing: order is signed. (See scanned order for complete details)
